subject: Re: Marginal / no resolution improvement with M
body:
Hi Matthias,

If you run a refinement in M and it fails to improve things then various parameters are now likely overfited -> you should revert both your M project and the tilt series metadata files in your warp project to their state prior to refinement in M.

In general, your ability to optimise a deformation model at a given resolution should depend on
- the number of particles per ‘multi-particle system’ (tilt-series for tomo, micrograph for spa)
- the distribution of particles in your field of view (i.e. don’t refine a 10x10 model if you have one particle in the upper left)
- the amount of signal you are trying to align on
- how well your reference matches your particles

Your tilt-series alignments are probably pretty good, how small is the protein you’re trying to align on? 
1.8e-/A2 per tilt image is a bit lower than the ‘usual’ 3e-/A2/tilt image, +-60 with 3deg increments which may also affect ‘alignability’ of particles from your tilt images. You also didn’t mention the number of temporal samples for particle poses, this is another factor to consider

If I was in your shoes, I would start by
1) reverting all metadata to pre-M
2) making sure that reconstructing subtomograms exported from warp gives me my 20A reconstruction before importing that metadata into M
3) refining a very simple model and seeing if it brings improvement (poses, 1x1 image warp, 1 temporal pose)

It may also be that you are limited by the flexibility of your protein, assessing the 3D variability in your data may help you to see how much of a problem this is. I’ve had some luck doing this in Dynamo on warp reconstructed subtomograms

Hope that helps a little, good luck!

Cheers

Alister

> On 3 Dec 2021, at 09:17, Matthias Vorländer <mvorlaender@gmail.com> wrote:
> 
> Hi guys, 
> 
> I am refining a tomo dataset of ~5000 particles from 109 tilt series of a somewhat flexible protein bound to some very heterogeneous additional density with Relion and M.  Data was collected on a K3, dose-symmetric tilt scheme, 120 e/Å total dose 2 degrees increment, 6 frames/movie (~0.3 e/A/frame). The sample was purified, so we have thin ice. We didn't have fiducials, so I used only patch tracking to align the tilt series - this seemed to have worked reasonably well because particles are huge and have good contrast (mean residual error from etomo log files is 1.45 Å). Subtomos are at a pixel size of 5Å/px, original data was at 1.38Å/px. I started with ~20Å resolution when I imported the data into M,  then did the following refinements
> First Refinement only poses and image warp grid with 3 x 3, one subiteration. "Improved" from 20.0Å to 19.8Å
> Second refinement with Image warp grid 9x6, Particle poses, Stage angles, Volume warp grid 4x6x2x10. This run crashed, so I tried again the same but without stage angles --> no improvement
> Next, only particle poses and Stage angles --> no improvement
> Next, again Image Warp with 6x4 & particle Poses --> No improvement
> I am new to this, so I have no idea what/how much resolution improvement can be expected with this number of particles and parameters? ANy advice would be much appreciated!
> 
> Thanks a lot,
> Matthias 
> 
> --
